summary: The task is complete in substance. The repo now has a dedicated `site-settings` domain with typed defaults, a singleton persisted Prisma model, a server-only repo/service boundary, and `/app/settings` wired into the owner workspace. Shared shell branding and root metadata both read through the same persisted settings service with deterministic fallbacks when no record exists, and demo-user writes are still blocked at the server boundary. The implementation reads as the start of a durable configuration domain rather than a route-local branding hack, and the required verification commands passed.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Documented and implemented settings foundation exists for site title and description.
Public and private shell branding read the same persisted settings values via the shared settings service, with safe defaults when no row exists.
The boundary is extensible: dedicated `src/features/site-settings` types/repo/service plus a singleton persistence model support later settings growth.
`npm run verify` passed in the provided deterministic check summary.
